content:
# Final Review Planner Positions
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.4-xhigh

## Summary

Three amendments identify real compatibility/diagnostics/resolver defects in source. The two run-state/resume amendments do not hold up because the cited reviewer pair matches the authoritative live project override, and resume drift detection already compares full reviewer identities before the minimum-count checks.

## Votes

- `fr-4-e6fe4b51`: Accept
  `backend show-effective` is documented as a stable DTO, and `final_reviewer` remains a named policy role in shared domain code, but diagnostics now remove that role from `roles` and `backend probe --role final_reviewer` now hard-fails. Replacing an existing role key/CLI target with only `final_review_panel.reviewer[i]` is a backward-incompatible contract break unless the surface is versioned or the old alias is preserved as compatibility output.
- `fr-4-0a3c4ba5`: Accept
  The new compiled Codex defaults are supplied through `backends.codex.role_models.implementer` and `backends.codex.role_models.final_reviewer`, but `model_source_for_role` suppresses role-model entries when their source is `Default` and falls through to generic `"default"`. On a fresh workspace that makes `implementer` and the Codex final-review reviewer report the wrong control point, which is a real diagnostics correctness issue.
- `fr-4-0b4daea6`: Accept
  `BackendResolver::apply_selection` resets the model with `ModelSpec::default_for_backend()` whenever the backend family changes, so layered selections can erase the role-specific implementer default from `BackendRole::Implementer.default_target()`. That leaves `BackendResolver` able to resolve `codex/gpt-5.4` where `BackendPolicyService` resolves `codex/gpt-5.4-high`, which is an avoidable correctness and maintenance split even if only legacy callers hit it today.
- `fr-4-837d9207`: Reject
  The cited `codex + codex-spark` reviewer pair is explained by the authoritative live project config at `.git/ralph-burning-live/projects/utm/config.toml`, which explicitly sets `final_review.backends = ["codex/gpt-5.4-xhigh", "codex/gpt-5.3-codex-spark-xhigh"]`. That is project-specific runtime state, not evidence that the compiled default reviewer pair or backend-default implementation is wrong.
- `fr-4-c605f8ed`: Reject
  The amendment focuses on `drift_still_satisfies_requirements`, but the resume path first calls `resolution_has_drifted()`, which compares the full `final_review_reviewers` vectors, including backend and model IDs, before the minimum-count/planner/arbiter checks run. A changed reviewer pair is therefore detected as drift; the cited snapshot is only reused because it matches the active live override, not because reviewer identity changes are ignored.
